Bearing 315

The newsletter of the Cascade Orienteering Club | April-June 2002
www.CascadeOC.org | Page 1

\ Executive Board Member*

Cascade Celebrates 25th Year

[Image: Black and white photo of four men standing in a forest holding a white "SILVA" banner. Caption below reads: Early Cascade organizers, circa 1980. From left: Tom Stewart, Einar Storackers, Len Englund, and Bo Powell.]

This year marks COC's 25th anniversary. In each issue of Bearing 315, we'll be printing historical photos and maps of early Cascade events. If you have any photos, maps, or historical accounts of Cascade's early days that you would like to see in the newsletter, please send them to me at the address below (or via e-mail at dnewel@gte.net).

> Debbie Newell
> 6216 N.E. 154th St.
> Kenmore WA 98028

We will also include a special 25th anniversary celebration at this year's annual club dinner, scheduled for November. If you have any brilliant ideas you would like to see included at the dinner or in the newsletter, please let me know.

The Ultimate Orienteer Series Continues

It's not too late to participate in the Ultimate Orienteer series, which has 3 events remaining: Score-O, Classic-O, and Long-O. On the longest course at each UO event, you receive points based on your finish time, with your best 3 scores added together to determine the male and female “Ultimate Orienteer.” See the schedule page for details about remaining Ultimate Orienteer events.

4 Cascade Members Named to US Orienteering Team

Four Cascade members were recently named to the 2002 US Standing Orienteering Team. Members of the Standing Team are eligible to be selected for World Cup and World Championship Teams.

Eric Bone, the current top-ranked US orienteer, was named to the men's “A” team, while Eileen Breseman, Jennifer Knowles, and Debbie Newell were named to the women's “C” team. Congratulations to all 4 US Team members.

New COC E-mail List

Thanks to Technical Coordinator Bill Cusworth, Cascade has joined the electronic age with a club e-mail list. The list will be used for upcoming event announcements, results of past events, carpool arrangements, and discussions about club business or orienteering in general. Although it is a Cascade OC club list, anyone interested in orienteering in the Puget Sound area will probably want to join in order to keep up to date on the latest event news and discussions. To limit spam, you must subscribe to the list in order to receive and post messages.

To subscribe to the list
Visit http://groups.yahoo.com/group/cascadeoc/ __PH_1__ Send e-mail to: cascadeoc-subscribe@yahoogroups.com

To post a message
cascadeoc@yahoogroups.com

To unsubscribe
cascadeoc-unsubscribe@yahoogroups.com

To contact the list owner
cascadeoc-owner@yahoogroups.com

Race Series Debuts

Justin Yeager, Cascade member and avid adventure racer, has put together a series of navigation races to be held in the Ellensburg area this spring and summer. Events in the Summit Series will include trail running, trekking, hiking, and cross country navigation.

Each event will test participants' physical stamina and mental toughness in some of the most rugged and magnificent terrain in Washington State. Participants, encouraged to race as teams, will find themselves fording rivers, ascending peaks, traversing ridge lines, descending into ravines, enduring the scorching desert heat or fighting the frigid rain of the Cascades, all in an effort to finish the race. Not all will complete the event; some will lose their way, and others will become physically or emotionally drained with no desire left.

2-Hour Navigational Challenge: 3-6 miles
This series is for beginners, those who wish to learn and have fun with wilderness navigation. Participants will have an opportunity to learn basic map and compass skills at a pre-race workshop and then test their skills while developing confidence navigating on a course that is shorter and less difficult than the 6-hour challenge.

6-Hour Navigational Challenge: 15-20+ miles
This series is designed to test the navigational ability and endurance of individuals and teams. Courses and race formats will be designed to produce circumstances similar to those encountered in an adventure race. It is strongly recommended that competitors be familiar with USGS topographic maps and compass use.

For more information or to register, please call Justin Yeager at 253-709-8232 or visit www.northwest-adventures.com.

Frequent O'er Card

[Image: Graphic of a "Frequent O'er Card" showing ten punched boxes, each labeled "$5".]

Prepay your entry to Cascade events and get a bonus, too. For $50, you will get a card like this. To pay for an event, just have one of the boxes punched. After you've used up all 10 boxes, give the card to the registrar for a bonus $5 meet credit. To purchase a card, contact Debbie Newell (425-488-3691, dnewel@gte.net). These cards are only valid at Cascade O' Club events.

Fort Casey Event Information

The Ft. Casey score-o event on April 6 will feature a mass start at 11:00 a.m. The information below should help you get to the meet on time.

Driving time from Clinton to Ft. Casey - 26 miles: allow 45 minutes (see map below).

Ferry Times

Mukilteo-Clinton
Sailing time 20 minutes

Any of the following boats should get you to Ft. Casey in time for the 11:00 a.m. mass start. You may have to wait for a boat, so take that into consideration.

6:00am 7:00am
8:00am 8:30am
9:00 am 9:30am (not much time to spare, though!)

Clinton-Mukilteo
Boats leave approximately every ½ hour until 10:30 p.m., and then at 11:30 and 12:30.

Current Fares

Vehicle & Driver
Regular $5.00 Senior/Disabled $4.30

Passengers
Regular $2.70 Senior/Disabled $1.30
Youth $1.90 Bicycle Surcharge $.90

Motorcycles and large vehicles have different fare structures.

President – vacant Vice President/Training Coordinator – Eric Bone, 206-634-2279, eric.bone@attbi.com Treasurer – Katie Sprugel, 206-365-8742, sprugels@att.net Secretary – Ardis Dull, 206-364-4062, ardisdull@juno.com Mapping Coordinator – Don Denbo, 206-366-0624, dwd6@mindspring.com Equipment Coordinator – Don Atkinson, 425-488-0270, ona@atmos.washington.edu Newsletter Editor – Debbie Newell, 425-488-3691, dnewel@gte.net Membership Coordinator – Jim Siscel, 425-778-7202, jsiscel@cmc.net Publicity/Promotion Coordinator – Jennifer Knowles, 253-845-6588, jsknowles@msn.com Land Use Coordinator - Dave Tallent, 425-488-3691, dtallent@gtemail.net Volunteer Coordinator – Kean Williams, 425-931-3036, keanwill@yahoo.com Technical Coordinator – Bill Cusworth, 206-271-0652, cusworth@chem.washington.edu Jan Urban, 425-402-7225, urbanj@molecumetics.com

[Image: Black and white photo of four men standing in a forest holding a white "SILVA" banner. Caption below reads: Early Cascade organizers, circa 1980. From left: Tom Stewart, Einar Storackers, Len Englund, and Bo Powell.]

This year marks COC's 25th anniversary. In each issue of Bearing 315, we'll be printing historical photos and maps of early Cascade events. If you have any photos, maps, or historical accounts of Cascade's early days that you would like to see in the newsletter, please send them to me at the address below (or via e-mail at dnewel@gte.net).
> Debbie Newell > 6216 N.E. 154th St. > Kenmore WA 98028

We will also include a special 25th anniversary celebration at this year's annual club dinner, scheduled for November. If you have any brilliant ideas you would like to see included at the dinner or in the newsletter, please let me know.

It's not too late to participate in the Ultimate Orienteer series, which has 3 events remaining: Score-O, Classic-O, and Long-O. On the longest course at each UO event, you receive points based on your finish time, with your best 3 scores added together to determine the male and female “Ultimate Orienteer.” See the schedule page for details about remaining Ultimate Orienteer events.
4 Cascade Members Named to US Orienteering Team
Four Cascade members were recently named to the 2002 US Standing Orienteering Team. Members of the Standing Team are eligible to be selected for World Cup and World Championship Teams.

Eric Bone, the current top-ranked US orienteer, was named to the men's “A” team, while Eileen Breseman, Jennifer Knowles, and Debbie Newell were named to the women's “C” team. Congratulations to all 4 US Team members.

Thanks to Technical Coordinator Bill Cusworth, Cascade has joined the electronic age with a club e-mail list. The list will be used for upcoming event announcements, results of past events, carpool arrangements, and discussions about club business or orienteering in general. Although it is a Cascade OC club list, anyone interested in orienteering in the Puget Sound area will probably want to join in order to keep up to date on the latest event news and discussions. To limit spam, you must subscribe to the list in order to receive and post messages.
To subscribe to the list Visit http://groups.yahoo.com/group/cascadeoc/ OR Send e-mail to: cascadeoc-subscribe@yahoogroups.com
To contact the list owner cascadeoc-owner@yahoogroups.com

Justin Yeager, Cascade member and avid adventure racer, has put together a series of navigation races to be held in the Ellensburg area this spring and summer. Events in the Summit Series will include trail running, trekking, hiking, and cross country navigation.

Each event will test participants' physical stamina and mental toughness in some of the most rugged and magnificent terrain in Washington State. Participants, encouraged to race as teams, will find themselves fording rivers, ascending peaks, traversing ridge lines, descending into ravines, enduring the scorching desert heat or fighting the frigid rain of the Cascades, all in an effort to finish the race. Not all will complete the event; some will lose their way, and others will become physically or emotionally drained with no desire left.

2-Hour Navigational Challenge: 3-6 miles This series is for beginners, those who wish to learn and have fun with wilderness navigation. Participants will have an opportunity to learn basic map and compass skills at a pre-race workshop and then test their skills while developing confidence navigating on a course that is shorter and less difficult than the 6-hour challenge.

6-Hour Navigational Challenge: 15-20+ miles This series is designed to test the navigational ability and endurance of individuals and teams. Courses and race formats will be designed to produce circumstances similar to those encountered in an adventure race. It is strongly recommended that competitors be familiar with USGS topographic maps and compass use.
For more information or to register, please call Justin Yeager at 253-709-8232 or visit www.northwest-adventures.com.
